1.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
1.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

1.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1300 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 7×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
1.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

1.name: Run Intervals — 4×1000m
1.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 4×1000m at 10K / tempo pace with 90 sec jog recovery between. Cool down 10 min. Focus on consistent splits, not all-out.

1.name: Swim Threshold — 1500 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
1.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

1.name: Tri Performance — Base Hypertrophy (Lower)
1.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Base Week 1 — Two primary lower compounds (65% 1RM, RIR 3) plus single-leg RDL 2×8/leg and Hip Thrusts 3×8-10 for glute drive; accessories stay light. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells.
